content:
Hi Ramadji

Could you kindly provide further information to enable us to assist you.

Some basics first.

  • Try restarting the app and your phone.
  • Confirm your phone has a working cellular / wifi data connection.
  • Check there's no temporary outage with your email provider.
  1. What version of Android OS and Thunderbird are you running?
  2. Did the issue begin after upgrading the app or Android version?
  3. Can you provide exact details of any error messages being displayed.
  4. What mail provider are you having difficulty with e.g Gmail, Hotmail, Outlook, Office365 etc.
  5. Do you have more than one account setup, if so, do the other accounts send/receive successfully?
  6. Is this a first time app install or a new fault that's developed on a previously working setup?

Greater detail will enable us to offer suggestions and attempt to replicate the fault where applicable.
creator: platform34

content:
Hi zbadger

Thunderbird for Android downloads emails from pop3 accounts and stores them on the phones internal storage. There's no way to backup, export or save them to another location on the phone.

Pop3 accounts can be configured so emails are either deleted from the server once downloaded to the email client, or always retained on the server. In comparison, emails downloaded via Imap are always retained on the server.

Your options depend on how you've setup the pop3. Are the emails stored on your phone your only copy, or does a copy also exist on the server?

If the phone holds your only copy, you will loose these emails if you remove the account on your phone. If you have all the emails stored on the server, they will download again to the app via Imap.

You could install the K9 email client on your phone. This is essentially the same app as Thunderbird and can co-exist at the same time. You could try setting up the imap account on K9 while not affecting the emails stored in Thunderbird.

Hi Firefox1748

Can you check your sent folder is correctly assigned, ie it's not set to none. Go to: Settings > Account > Folders

The Thunderbird team and wider community might have further suggestions.

Assuming you're now using v13, your quickest resolution might be to remove and add the account again. You can export your account settings to a file and use this to restore the account after removal. Go to: Settings > Export / Import.

Your emails will download again once you complete the account login.

To remove an account Go to settings > account > three dot menu > remove.

content:
Hi Rancepete

Unfortunately Thunderbird for Android doesn't have a print function at this time.

It is a feature that's been requested by the community. Please consider up-voting the idea on Mozilla connect. The Thunderbird team take these into consideration when planning their road-maps and priorities.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/ability-to-print-email-messages/idi-p/71885

For now, a possible workaround is to take a screen capture of the email. You could then open this in another app which supports printing. Alternatively open the email in your account providers web portal and print from there. I think Android browsers support printing via the "share" feature.

Sie können festlegen, dass E-Mails sofort zugestellt werden, anstatt sie regelmäßig zu synchronisieren.

Um dies zu aktivieren...

content:
Hi Ksands

Message downloads via imap are not deleted from the server.

If you however delete emails using the app, they will be deleted from the server, ie. moved to the servers deleted/trash folder. Permanent deletion would require you to empty the trash folder.

You can set how deletions are handled within the app. Settings > Account > Fetching Mail: [When I delete a message]

  • Do not delete from server
  • Delete from server
  • Mark as read on server

Did you manually add the server settings or use the automatically detected ones.

To re-assure yourself, perhaps double check Thunderbird on your phone is setup to use Imap and not pop. Look at settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server.
creator: platform34

content:
My goal is to be able to read ALL of my emails on my phone and control when they are deleted on the server from my POP3 laptop client. So I changed a setting on my POP3 laptop under settings>account settings > server settings:

1. Leave messages on server - This was unchecked and I checked it. I also checked: For at most 1 day.

By doing this, I assume messages will stay on the server until:

1. I delete a message 2. If I have not manually deleted a message, it will be deleted automatically after a day on the server.

On my IMAP phone client under Settings > Account > Fetching mail I left the following settings as is:

1. Sync server deletions - This is checked. 2. Mark as read when deleted - This is checked. Not sure how to interpret this. Is this mark as read on Server or on my phone ? 3. When I delete a message - The option is set to Delete from server 4. Erase deleted messages on server - This option is set to Immediately.

With these settings I assume I will see ALL messages on my phone, and they will disappear from my phone when I either delete a message from my POP3 laptop client, OR the messages are deleted automatically on the server after a day based on my POP3 laptop client settings.

As to whether my phone client is setup as an IMAP client: When I go to Settings > Account > Fetching mail > Incoming server, there is no definitive setting that says IMAP. The ONLY setting I see that mentions IMAP is: Auto-detect IMAP namespace and that setting is checked. After googling how to setup IMAP on my Thunderbird phone client, I found that setting the port SEEMED to control whether it is IMAP. I am not using any security for my emails, so I am using port 143 with the same server, authentication, username, and password as my POP3 laptop client. The only difference is I am using port 143 for IMAP on my phone and port 110 for POP3 on my laptop client.


If I am misinterpreting these settings, please let me know.

content:
Hi ksands

Emails deleted as you describe will be removed from the phone.

You will see ALL messages ONLY from folders that "Sync" or "Push" is enabled on. If sync is not enabled on a folder, the contained messages will not download.

To check the folders being synchronised. Navigate: Email account > manage folders > [folder name]

Regarding your question on the "Mark as read when deleted" option. This setting changes the email status to unread before moving it to the phone and servers deleted/trash folder.

You don't say who your email provider is! Imap on 143 for non TLS/SSL connections appears common. Using a secure connection is highly advised.

content:
They are looking in to this issue, but it has been noticed that this sometimes occurs when people have a deeper hierachy of folders/subfolders. It seems a possibility it's related to the number of characters in the path name.

In the meantime suggestion is too try this first: If you have any folders with a more lengthy name, then rename to a shorter name: eg: If folder name is 'Online Orders' then rename to 'Orders' as that saves 7 chars (including the space) Or if you have a folder called eg: 'Marks and Spencer' rename as 'M&S'

Some people may have an issue if they got Thunderbird via the 'Windows App Store' because Windows auot puts the profile in a location which has a longer path directory. If this is your case, then it's advised to uninstall Thunderbird and get a download from the official website: https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

You can move the profile if required or set up again in a new profile. Info can be supplied if required.

content:
Hi Ole,

It sounds like Thunderbird isn’t properly saving your profile or local folder settings between sessions. Here are a few things you can check:

1. **Profile location:** 

  * Go to **Help → More Troubleshooting Information → Profile Folder → Open Folder**.   * Make sure your *Local Folders* data (usually in `Mail/Local Folders`) is actually there and not being stored in a temporary or roaming location.

2. **Check permissions:** 

  * Ensure that the profile folder isn’t marked as *read-only* and that you have full write permissions to it.

3. **Antivirus or cleaner software:** 

  * Some antivirus or “system cleaner” tools can remove or block Thunderbird data. Try adding Thunderbird’s profile folder to your antivirus exclusion list.

4. **Rebuild your profile (if needed):** 

  * Close Thunderbird.   * Copy the contents of your `Mail/Local Folders` folder somewhere safe.   * Create a new Thunderbird profile using the **Profile Manager** (`thunderbird.exe -P`), then move your saved Local Folders data into the new profile’s `Mail/Local Folders` folder.

After that, restart Thunderbird and check if the folders persist after closing and reopening.

Kind regards, Rahmat

content:
Hi Stefob

Yes, you can set the folders you wish to sync (refresh).

1. Press the top left menu button 2. Select account 3. Select manage folders (bottom left) 4. Click the desired folder 5. Toggle "Sync" ON

The sync period is defined and applied at the account level. Go to: Settings > account > fetching mail: Folder Poll Frequency (default = 15 minutes)

You also have the option to enable push per folder for immediately delivery.

Guide: https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't currently have an in-app feature to add new imap folders.

This needs to be done via your email providers web portal or an alternative client that supports this. Once the folder is created, refresh the apps folder list from: manage folders > three dot menu > refresh folders.

Members in the community have requested this feature. Consider up-voting their suggestion on the Mozilla Connect link below.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/add-the-ability-to-create-imap-folders/idi-p/72777

It just leaves the email in the outbox, gives the error and sends from Entwürfe the second time without error, but with two entries in sent folder.

I will think of a different way to reproduce the issue...

content: That does sound frustrating, Gerrit. Thanks for explaining the issue in detail — that really helps. It seems like a syncing problem with the outbox on Thunderbird mobile. You could try clearing the app’s cache or checking for the latest update; that often fixes similar issues. Hopefully, the developers can patch this soon so sending emails works smoothly again.
